Billing confirmed: PayPal on file, R2 Paid Active, no free-tier wall. Watchdog/cap exist BECAUSE of HF throttle -> candidates for removal on R2.

GC item DONE EARLY (forced, release-day): watchdog disabled to no-op (commit a780e0a). It crashed the whole server (code 1) via ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END when HF throttled gemma-clip to 101KB/s + bogus best=6554MB/s. Method body left for R2 task to delete fully (+ _bestSpeed/_slowSince/_slowReconnect* fields + SLOW_RECONNECT_* consts). CORRECTION: verified master never had the watchdog. add 02ef2c0 + disable a780e0a are BOTH RunPod-only (git branch --contains 02ef2c0 = RunPod; git show master:downloadManager.js = no watchdog markers). So master (1.0.1) never had the crash bug -> NO cherry-pick needed. Earlier card item (b) saying carry a780e0a to master was wrong, fixed. Public 1.0.2 just needs R2 links + bump; watchdog rides along as net no-op via promote or gets GC-removed.

All 9 LTX weights uploaded to cubric-builds/models/ltx-2.3/ and verified (rclone size match + public HEAD 200 + Content-Length match): the 8 non-transformer files (~22GB) + the 41GB transformer (uploaded after it STALLED on HF during a live Pod test — confirming the throttle). dependencies.js: all 9 LTX url fields swapped huggingface.co -> dl.cubric.studio; sha256 unchanged (files byte-identical, no re-hash). LTX path now 100% R2, ZERO HF. Live result: R2 served files FLAT at ~200MB/s (217MB/s observed) vs HF sawtooth 20-27MB/s that stalled. STILL OPEN on this card: (a) the OTHER MPI re-hosts not part of LTX (Wan, Singularity, gemma-heretic base, ID-LoRA, lipdub) still on HF -> migrate per dep re-host rule; (b) rclone upload SCRIPT extension (models/ prefix automation) not done — this was a manual one-off upload; (c) land on master + bump 1.0.2 for public launch; (d) GC review of slow-stream watchdog/concurrency cap once ALL HF urls gone (LTX done, others remain).
